title: Konda Reddy Fort
tagline: Konda Reddy Fort — heritage city of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Konda Reddy Fort, also known as Kondareddy Buruju is a fort situated in the city of Kurnool in Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Konda Reddy Fort, also known as Kondareddy Buruju is a fort situated in the city of Kurnool in Andhra Pradesh, India. At a distance of 2 km from Kurnool Railway Station, 2.9 km from Kurnool New Bus Stand and 24 km from Alampur, Konda Reddy fort is an imposing structure situated in the heart of Kurnool City. The monument, a semi-circular bastion crowned with a tower, is the only extant remains of an old fortification around the city.

topPlaces.name: Kurnool
topPlaces.description: Kurnool is a city in the state of Andhra Pradesh, India. It formerly served as the capital of Andhra State (1953–1956).

topPlaces.name: Kurnool Urban mandal
topPlaces.description: Kurnool Urban mandal is one of the 27 mandals in Kurnool district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is administered under Kurnool revenue division and its headquarters are located at Kurnool.

topPlaces.name: Kurnool bus station
topPlaces.description: Kurnool bus station is a bus station located in Kurnool city of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is owned by Andhra Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Kurnool
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Kurnool (Latin: Dioecesis Kurnoolensis) is a diocese located in the city of Kurnool in the ecclesiastical province of Hyderabad in India.

topPlaces.name: Jwalapuram
topPlaces.description: Jwalapuram (meaning "City of fire" in Telugu) is an archaeological site in the Nandyal district of Andhra Pradesh, southern India, which shows hominid habitation before and after the Toba event (73 kya) according to the Toba catastrophe theory. It is unclear what species of humans settled Jwalapuram as no fossil remains have yet been found.

topPlaces.name: Andhra State
topPlaces.description: Andhra State (IAST: Āndhra Rāṣṭramu; [ˈɑːndʰrʌ raːʂʈɾamu]), created in 1953, was the official name of the State of Andhra Pradesh until 1956. The state was formed from Telugu-speaking districts of the erstwhile Madras State, which form two distinct cultural regions – Rayalaseema and Coastal Andhra.

topPlaces.name: Swayambhu Sri Abhista Gnana Ganapathi Temple
topPlaces.description: The Swayambhu Sri Abhista Gnana Ganapathi Temple (SSAGG Temple) is a Ganapathi temple located in Mayuri Greenland, Kurnool district, Andhra Pradesh, India.

image.alt: Swayambhu Sri Abhista Gnana Ganapathi Temple

topPlaces.name: Alampur Papanasi Temples
topPlaces.description: Alampuram Papanasi Temples are a group of twenty three Hindu temples dated between 9th- and 11th-century that have been relocated to the southwest of Alampuram village in Telangana. This cluster of mostly ruined temples are co-located near the meeting point of Tungabhadra River and Krishna River at the border of Andhra Pradesh.

items.note: Kurnool is a city in the state of Andhra Pradesh, India. It formerly served as the capital of Andhra State (1953–1956).
Kurnool Urban mandal is one of the 27 mandals in Kurnool district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is administered under Kurnool revenue division and its headquarters are located at Kurnool.
Kurnool bus station is a bus station located in Kurnool city of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is owned by Andhra Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation.
The Roman Catholic Diocese of Kurnool (Latin: Dioecesis Kurnoolensis) is a diocese located in the city of Kurnool in the ecclesiastical province of Hyderabad in India.

Jwalapuram (meaning "City of fire" in Telugu) is an archaeological site in the Nandyal district of Andhra Pradesh, southern India, which shows hominid habitation before and after the Toba event (73 kya) according to the Toba catastrophe theory. It is unclear what species of humans settled Jwalapuram as no fossil remains have yet been found.

Andhra State (IAST: Āndhra Rāṣṭramu; [ˈɑːndʰrʌ raːʂʈɾamu]), created in 1953, was the official name of the State of Andhra Pradesh until 1956. The state was formed from Telugu-speaking districts of the erstwhile Madras State, which form two distinct cultural regions – Rayalaseema and Coastal Andhra.

items.activity: Swayambhu Sri Abhista Gnana Ganapathi Temple
items.note: The Swayambhu Sri Abhista Gnana Ganapathi Temple (SSAGG Temple) is a Ganapathi temple located in Mayuri Greenland, Kurnool district, Andhra Pradesh, India.

Alampuram Papanasi Temples are a group of twenty three Hindu temples dated between 9th- and 11th-century that have been relocated to the southwest of Alampuram village in Telangana. This cluster of mostly ruined temples are co-located near the meeting point of Tungabhadra River and Krishna River at the border of Andhra Pradesh.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Konda Reddy Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Konda Reddy Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Konda Reddy Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kurnool Airport (14 km) and the nearest railway station is Kacheguda (Hyderabad) (180 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Konda Reddy Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Konda Reddy Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Konda Reddy Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Konda Reddy Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Konda Reddy Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kurnool, Kurnool Urban mandal, Kurnool bus station, Diocese of Kurnool, Jwalapuram.
